skill.name: plugin-forge
skill.description: Create and manage Claude Code plugins with scaffolding scripts, manifest guidance, version updates, and marketplace workflow references.
skill.summary: Build Claude Code plugins with correct manifests, structure, and marketplace registration.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and weak-crypto patterns, but most are markdown examples, JSON snippets, or literal plugin paths. The confirmed risk is local filesystem mutation by Python helper scripts, including an unsanitized plugin name used in output paths. No evidence found of credential exfiltration, runtime network access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized Plugin Name Used in Filesystem Paths
medium_findings.description: The create script accepts plugin_name from the command line and joins it into the plugin output path without enforcing kebab-case or rejecting path separators. A crafted name could create plugin files outside the intended plugins directory when the script is run on a trusted marketplace root.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is built directly from user-controlled plugin_name, and no validation is present near argument parsing. The impact is limited because the script is a local developer tool rather than exposed network service.

medium_findings.title: Helper Scripts Modify Local Marketplace Files
medium_findings.description: The included scripts create directories, write plugin manifests, generate README files, and rewrite marketplace manifests under a user-supplied marketplace root. This is intended behavior, but users should run it only in trusted repositories after reviewing the target path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and central to the tool workflow. They are legitimate for plugin scaffolding but still materially alter local project files.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The numerous external command alerts are from bash blocks and slash-command examples in markdown documentation. The Python scripts do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell execution APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced markdown examples, and source review found no executable shell invocation in the Python files. This makes the static command alerts likely false positives.

low_findings.title: Network Alert Is a Non-Executable Schema Example
low_findings.description: The only network indicator is an example git URL inside marketplace schema documentation. No runtime HTTP client, socket use, or network request code was found in the executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a JSON example and is not referenced by executable code. Search of the scripts found no network libraries or calls.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts point to documentation text, markdown paths, and ordinary identifiers rather than cryptographic function calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithms used by the scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines describe plugin workflows or function signatures, not cryptographic operations. The reviewed Python code does not import or call cryptographic APIs.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Path Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The script constructs local output paths from command-line values without validating plugin_name against the documented kebab-case convention.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction and argument definition are directly visible. The risk depends on a user or AI passing an unsafe plugin name.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project Manifest Rewrite
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts rewrite plugin and marketplace JSON files, which is expected for this skill but requires trusted target directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly loads and writes manifest files. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so it is a controlled risk rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Plugin authors need consistent structure, manifests, and version updates across local marketplaces. This skill provides scaffolding scripts, schema references, and workflows for Claude Code plugin development.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold Claude Code plugin folders with commands and skills directories.
Generate a plugin.json manifest from author, description, keyword, and category inputs.
Append new plugin entries to an existing marketplace.json catalog.
Bump major, minor, or patch versions in plugin and marketplace manifests.
Explain supported plugin components, including commands, agents, hooks, skills, and MCP files.
Guide local testing, reinstall, and publishing workflows for plugin marketplaces.
limitations: Requires Python 3.10 or newer for the included scripts.
Requires an existing marketplace.json before creating a plugin entry.
Does not validate plugin names against a strict safe path pattern.
Does not publish plugins automatically to a remote marketplace.

use_cases.title: Create a First Plugin
use_cases.description: Generate the required plugin folders, manifest, README, and local marketplace entry for a new Claude Code plugin.

use_cases.title: Maintain Marketplace Versions
use_cases.description: Keep plugin.json and marketplace.json versions synchronized when shipping fixes, features, or breaking changes.

use_cases.title: Document Plugin Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the references to standardize command, skill, hook, agent, and MCP layout across team plugins.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude Code plugin named project-tools in this marketplace. Use my name and email, add productivity keywords, and explain each generated file.

prompt_templates.title: Add Plugin Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add a namespaced command and a skill to my existing plugin. Follow the plugin structure reference and update documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plugin changes, choose the correct semantic version bump, and update both plugin.json and marketplace.json.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Marketplace Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable workflow for our team to create, test, version, and publish Claude Code plugins in a shared marketplace.

output_examples.input: Create a new plugin for API testing utilities.
output: A plugin folder is created with manifest, commands, skills, and README locations.
The marketplace catalog receives a new entry for the plugin.
The response lists next steps for adding commands and testing locally.

output_examples.input: Bump my plugin after adding a new command.
output: The assistant recommends a minor version bump for the feature addition.
Both manifest locations are updated to the same version.
The response summarizes the old version, new version, and files changed.

output_examples.input: Explain where to put hooks and MCP configuration.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the expected hook configuration path, the MCP configuration location, and how paths resolve from the plugin root.

best_practices: Use simple kebab-case plugin and command names before running scaffolding scripts.
Review the target marketplace root before allowing scripts to write files.
Test locally and reinstall the plugin before publishing or sharing it.
anti_patterns: Do not pass names containing slashes, dot segments, or absolute paths to scaffolding scripts.
Do not edit only one manifest when changing a plugin version.
Do not publish before checking generated README and marketplace metadata.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Claude Code plugin structure, plugin manifest, README template, and marketplace entry.

faq.question: Does it support Codex?
faq.answer: The marketplace content supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code discovery, but the workflows focus on Claude Code plugins.

faq.question: Can it update existing plugin versions?
faq.answer: Yes. The version script updates both plugin.json and marketplace.json for major, minor, or patch releases.

faq.question: Does it run network requests?
faq.answer: No runtime network code was found. The only URL is a documentation example for marketplace schema formats.

faq.question: What should I check before running scripts?
faq.answer: Check the marketplace root and plugin name. Avoid path separators or dot segments in plugin names.

faq.question: Can it publish plugins automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents publishing workflows but does not push code or publish to a remote service by itself.
